  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 3000.0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Sling tension in each leg is 3,000 lbs, which exceeds the rated capacity of the slings at a 30-degree angle."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not proceed with the lift using this configuration.",
    "Increase the horizontal sling angle (e.g., use longer slings) or use slings with a higher Working Load Limit (WLL)."
  ],
  "rationale": "The load is 3,000 lbs, shared equally by two legs, resulting in a vertical load of 1,500 lbs per leg. At a 30-degree horizontal sling angle, the tension in each leg is calculated as 1,500 lbs / sin(30°) = 3,000 lbs. The rated capacity (WLL) of each sling leg at a 30-degree angle is only 2,000 lbs (4,000 lbs vertical rating * sin(30°)). Since the actual tension of 3,000 lbs exceeds the rated capacity of 2,000 lbs at this angle, the configuration is unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
